description: Docker compute worker resource limits, all-state lifecycle inspection, dry-run cleanup, and Docker hygiene diagnostics.

userStories.title: Add compute worker resource policy and Docker run tests
userStories.description: As a compute engineer, I want Docker run command construction tested for resource limits so that worker safety is enforced before integration changes run on a host.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a compute resource policy model with defaults for memory, memory-swap, pids-limit, shm-size, init, restart policy, and log options
Add documented override sources for larger hosts without changing safe defaults
Add command-stub tests for one-time worker and dev worker Docker run arguments
Tests assert resource policy labels or inspectable metadata are included in Docker run configuration
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented compute resource policy model in src/compute/resource-policy.ts with conservative defaults, spawn-time env overrides, Docker run policy args, and inspectable resource labels. Refactored one-time and dev worker Docker run construction through exported helpers that apply the policy. Documented override sources in docs/specs/capabilities/docker-compute-workers.md. Added test/compute-resource-policy.test.mjs covering defaults, env overrides, policy args, one-time worker run args, dev worker run args, and policy labels. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md: focused Docker-worker tests passed; compute spawn help real CLI path checked; npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Apply Docker resource limits to one-time and dev workers
userStories.description: As an operator, I want every compute worker to start with host-safe Docker limits so that browser leaks are contained inside the worker.
acceptanceCriteria: Update one-time and dev worker spawn paths to include memory, memory-swap, pids-limit, shm-size, init, restart=no or equivalent, and bounded log options
Worker labels expose resource policy values, owner scope, role, created time, worktree, port block, TTL/idle values, and Ralph ids when applicable
OOM or PID-limit failures inside a worker do not configure Docker to restart it in a loop
Real-path Docker smoke check starts a limited worker and verifies basic gateway or shell access when Docker is available; if not feasible in unit tests, document the integration command
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented worker lifecycle metadata labels and CLI/plumbing for TTL, idle, owner-scope, worktree/path, port block/ports, and optional Ralph job/run ids on both one-time and dev worker Docker run paths while preserving enforced resource limits, restart=no, --init, shm, PID, memory/swap, and bounded log options. Added focused tests for one-time and dev worker Docker args plus lifecycle label defaults/overrides. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md: focused Docker-worker tests passed, compute spawn/dev spawn help showed new options, current Docker dev worker read-only inspect confirmed limited HostConfig with restart=no and bounded logs, full npm run typecheck passed. Real new-worker smoke creation was not run because this Ralph task forbids creating/releasing Docker workers; documented the manual integration command in docker-compute-workers.md.

userStories.title: Implement all-state compute worker listing
userStories.description: As an operator, I want Pibo to list running and stopped worker containers so that OOM-killed and retained debug containers are not hidden.
acceptanceCriteria: Add or extend compute listing with an --all mode that includes running, exited, dead, restarting, and OOM-killed Pibo compute containers
Output includes container id/name, role, status, OOM flag when available, owner, worktree, Ralph ids, created time, last-used time, resource policy, ports when available, and cleanup eligibility
Text output has a clear empty state and suggests next diagnostic commands
JSON output exposes stable fields for agents and monitoring
Tests cover running, stopped, OOM-killed, no-port stopped container, and empty-state fixtures
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented all-state compute worker listing with Docker ps --all plus inspect enrichment for running/exited/dead/restarting/OOM-killed Pibo containers, stable JSON fields for state/status/OOM/ports/owner/worktree/Ralph/resource policy/cleanup eligibility, and text output with OOM/resource/cleanup columns plus empty-state next-command guidance. Added fixtures covering running, stopped, OOM-killed, no-port stopped, and empty-state rendering. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md; focused compute tests, read-only real host compute list --all text/JSON validation, and full Docker-worker npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Add dry-run compute reap planning for stopped, dirty, old, and dev workers
userStories.description: As an operator, I want cleanup preview output before deleting containers so that debug state and worktrees are not removed accidentally.
acceptanceCriteria: Reap builds a cleanup plan before destructive actions and supports dry-run or preview output
Selectors cover one-time workers, dev workers when explicitly included, stopped containers, dirty workers, and max-age filters
Plan output explains why each worker is eligible or skipped
Apply removes selected containers but does not delete Git worktrees unless an explicit worktree cleanup option or command is used
Tests cover dry-run, apply, dev exclusion by default, include-dev behavior, stopped container cleanup, dirty worker cleanup, and worktree preservation
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented plan-first compute reap cleanup: pibo compute reap now dry-runs by default, supports --apply for removal, --include-dev for dev containers, --no-stopped/--no-dirty selector control, max-age selection, JSON/text plan output with selected/skipped reasons, and worktree preservation messaging. Added dirty cleanup labels to worker parsing/listing and pure planning/apply helpers with fake-release test seam. Tests cover dry-run, apply, dev exclusion by default, include-dev behavior, stopped cleanup, dirty/OOM cleanup, max-age selection, skipped reasons, and worktree preservation. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md; focused compute tests, real read-only host dry-run JSON/text validation, Docker-worker CLI help validation, and full npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Add Docker build context and disk hygiene diagnostics
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want Docker build context and cache diagnostics so that few image tags do not hide large retained layers or BuildKit cache.
acceptanceCriteria: Update .dockerignore to exclude .worktrees, local browser-use profile/state, screenshots, logs, and Pibo local state that should not enter images
Add diagnostics that report Docker image size, container size, local volume size, build-cache size, and reclaimable bytes when Docker is available
Diagnostics distinguish container cleanup, image cleanup, build-cache prune, and worktree cleanup suggestions
Diagnostics are read-only by default and provide JSON output
Tests or fixtures cover parsed Docker disk usage and safe next-command rendering
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented Docker build context hygiene and read-only compute disk diagnostics. .dockerignore now excludes .worktrees, Pibo local state, browser-use/profile state, screenshots, logs, and browser/debug artifacts without excluding source UI assets. Added pibo compute diagnostics (alias disk) with text/JSON output for Docker image, container, local volume, BuildKit cache, byte/reclaimable counts, Docker-unavailable handling, and cleanup suggestions separated across container cleanup, image cleanup, build-cache prune, and worktree cleanup. Added parsing/rendering fixtures in test/compute-resource-policy.test.mjs. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md: focused Docker-worker tests passed, Docker-worker unavailable-path CLI validation passed, read-only real host Docker diagnostics returned images/containers/localVolumes/buildCache rows and suggestions, and full npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Validate limited worker behavior with real Docker when available
userStories.description: As an operator, I want an integration validation path proving that limited workers can still run Pibo checks while remaining bounded.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a documented integration command or test script that spawns a limited compute worker and verifies connect/gateway or shell access
Validation includes a browser-use or Chromium smoke path when the environment supports Docker and browser dependencies
Validation records resource policy values from Docker inspect or compute list output
If Docker is unavailable in CI, the test skips with a clear message and the manual command remains documented
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added scripts/compute-limited-worker-smoke.mjs and npm script compute:limited-worker-smoke. The script defaults to read-only dry-run, skips clearly when Docker is unavailable, and when explicitly run with --apply it spawns a bounded one-time worker, verifies shell access, runs a bounded Chromium smoke path, records Docker inspect resource policy values plus compute list visibility, and releases the worker in a finally cleanup path. Documented the script and equivalent manual commands in docs/specs/capabilities/docker-compute-workers.md; updated tasks. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md: focused Docker-worker compute tests passed, Docker-worker dry-run and Docker-unavailable skip paths passed, host read-only dry-run passed, and full npm run typecheck passed. Real --apply creation was not run in this Ralph session because the task explicitly forbids creating/releasing Docker workers.
